# **1\. Database Overview**

**Database Name: habit\_hero.db**

**Database Version: 1.0**

\[Brief description of what data the database stores and why.\]

# **2\. Entity Definitions**

Define each database table/entity below. Add more tables as needed.

## **Entity 1: habits**

| Field | Type | Constraints | Description |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **id** | INTEGER | PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT | Unique identifier for each habit |
| **name** | TEXT | NOT NULL | Habit title |
| **description** | TEXT |  | Optional details about the habit |
| **category\_id** | INTEGER | REFERENCES categories(id) | Links habit to a category |
| **priority** | INTEGER | DEFAULT 1 | Priority level (1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High) |
| **start\_date** | TEXT |  | Date the habit begins |
| **end\_date** | TEXT |  | Optional end date |
| **schedule\_type** | TEXT |  | Habit frequency (daily, weekly, custom) |
| **created\_at** | TEXT | NOT NULL | ISO 8601 timestamp when habit was created |
| **updated\_at** | TEXT |  | ISO 8601 timestamp when habit was last updated |

## **Entity 2: categories**

| Field | Type | Constraints | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **id** | INTEGER | PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT | Unique identifier for each category |
| **name** | TEXT | NOT NULL | Category name (e.g., Health, Study) |
| **icon** | TEXT | NOT NULL | User-selected icon reference (e.g., fitness\_center) |
| **color** | TEXT | NOT NULL | Hex color code for category display |
| **created\_at** | TEXT | NOT NULL | ISO 8601 timestamp when category was created |
| **updated\_at** | TEXT |  | ISO 8601 timestamp when category was last updated |

## **Entity 3: daily\_logs**

| Field | Type | Constraints | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **id** | INTEGER | PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT | Unique identifier for each log |
| **habit\_id** | INTEGER | REFERENCES habits(id) | Associated habit |
| **date** | TEXT | NOT NULL | Date of the log |
| **status** | INTEGER |  | Completion status (0=missed, 1=completed) |
| **value** | REAL |  | Numeric value for measurable habits |
| **notes** | TEXT |  | Optional notes |
| **created\_at** | TEXT | NOT NULL | ISO 8601 timestamp when category was created |
| **updated\_at** | TEXT |  | ISO 8601 timestamp when category was last updated |

## **Entity 4: streaks**

| Field | Type | Constraints | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **habit\_id** | INTEGER | PRIMARY KEY, REFERENCES habits(id) | Associated habit |
| **current\_streak** | INTEGER |  | Current consecutive completions |
| **longest\_streak** | INTEGER |  | Highest streak achieved |
| **last\_completed\_date** | TEXT |  | Last completion date |
| **created\_at** | TEXT | NOT NULL | ISO 8601 timestamp |
| **updated\_at** | TEXT |  | ISO 8601 timestamp |

## 

## **Entity 5: habit\_schedule**	

| Field | Type | Constraints | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **id** | INTEGER | PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT | Unique identifier |
| **habit\_id** | INTEGER | REFERENCES habits(id) | Associated habit |
| **day\_of\_week** | INTEGER | NOT NULL | Scheduled day (1=Mon … 7=Sun) |
| **created\_at** | TEXT | NOT NULL | ISO 8601 timestamp |
| **updated\_at** | TEXT |  | ISO 8601 timestamp |

## **Entity 6: reminders**

| Field | Type | Constraints | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **id** | INTEGER | PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT | Unique identifier |
| **habit\_id** | INTEGER | REFERENCES habits(id) | Associated habit |
| **reminder\_time** | TEXT | NOT NULL | Time for notification |
| **is\_enabled** | INTEGER |  | Reminder status (1=on, 0=off) |
| **created\_at** | TEXT | NOT NULL | ISO 8601 timestamp |
| **updated\_at** | TEXT |  | ISO 8601 timestamp |

# **3\. Relationships**

| Entity A | Relationship | Entity B | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **categories** | One-to-Many | habits | One category can contain multiple habits. |
| **habits** | One-to-Many | daily\_logs | One habit can have many daily completion records. |
| **habits** | One-to-One | streaks | Each habit has one streak record to track progress. |
| **habits** | One-to-Many | habit\_schedule | A habit can have multiple scheduled days. |
| **habits** | One-to-Many | reminders | A habit can have one or more reminder settings. |
| **habits** | Many-to-One | categories | Many habits belong to one category. |
| **daily\_logs** | Many-to-One | habits | Many daily logs belong to one habit. |
| **habit\_schedule** | Many-to-One | habits | Multiple schedule entries belong to one habit. |
| **reminders** | Many-to-One | habits | Multiple reminders can belong to one habit. |
| **streaks** | One-to-One | habits | Each streak record corresponds to one habit. |

**Simplified Relationship** 

| Entity A | Relationship | Entity B | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **categories** | One-to-Many  | habits | Categories group multiple habits. |
| **habits** | One-to-Many | daily\_logs | Habits generate daily tracking records. |
| **habits** | One-to-One | streaks | Each habit maintains one streak record. |
| **habits** | One-to-Many | habit\_schedule | Habits can repeat on multiple days. |
| **habits** | One-to-Many | reminders | Habits can have reminders set. |

# **4\. ERD Diagram**


![ERD Diagram](../assets/screenshots/ERD.png)

# **5\. SQLite Implementation Notes**

## **5.1 Database Helper Pattern**

The HabitHero app uses a **DatabaseHelper** class to manage the SQLite database.  
This class follows the **Singleton Pattern**, which means only one database instance is used in the entire app.

This helps the app to:

* Avoid duplicate database connections

* Improve performance

* Keep data consistent

The DatabaseHelper handles:

* Database creation

* Table creation

* Database upgrades

* CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete)

**Singleton Implementation**

The Singleton pattern ensures that only one object of a class exists.  
In HabitHero, this means there is only one database connection used by all parts of the app.

**How It Works**

* The constructor is private so no other class can create a new instance.

* A static instance is created and shared.

* The database is initialized only once.

![][image2]

In the code the **instance** represents the single shared **DatabaseHelper** used across the application, while **\_database** stores the active database connection for reuse. The private **\_init()** constructor prevents multiple objects from being created, enforcing the Singleton pattern. The **get database** method returns the existing connection or creates it if it does not exist. This ensures the app uses one consistent database connection, improving performance and resource management.

**Database Initialization**

Database initialization prepares the database so the app can store and retrieve data.

When the app starts, the DatabaseHelper checks if the database already exists. If it exists, the app opens it. If it does not exist, the app creates a new database file and sets up the required tables.

**Initialization Process**

1. The app gets the storage path of the device.

2. The database file **habit\_hero.db** is created if it does not exist.

3. The database is opened for use.

4. The onCreate function runs to create tables (first run only).

![][image3]

Database initialization is important because it ensures that the required tables exist before any data is saved. It helps prevent application crashes that may occur when the system tries to access missing tables or structures. Initialization also prepares the database for CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations, allowing the app to function properly. Additionally, it supports future upgrades through database versioning, making it easier to modify the database structure without losing existing data.

**Table Creation SQL**

When the database is created for the first time, SQL commands are executed to create the necessary tables.

Each table stores specific data needed by the HabitHero app.

* categories \- stores category details  
* habits \- stores habit information  
* daily\_logs \- stores daily progress  
* streaks \- stores streak data  
* habit\_schedule \- stores repeating days  
* reminders \- stores reminder 

**CRUD Method Signatures**

The DatabaseHelper provides methods to create, read, update, and delete data.

**Category CRUD**

**![][image4]**

**createCategory()**

* Adds a new category to the database  
* Example: user creates “Health” category  
* Returns the ID of the new record

**getCategories()**

* Retrieves all categories from the database  
* Used to display categories in the app

**updateCategory()**

* Updates category details such as name, icon, or color  
* Used when user edits a category

**deleteCategory()**

* Removes a category from the database  
* Used when user deletes a category

**Habit CRUD Methods**

**![][image5]**

**createHabit()**

* Saves a new habit to the database  
* Example: “Drink Water Daily”

**getHabits()**

* Retrieves all habits  
* Used to display habits on the dashboard

**updateHabit()**

* Updates habit details such as schedule or category  
* Used when user edits a habit

**deleteHabit()**

* Removes a habit from the database  
* Used when user deletes a habit

**Daily Log CRUD Methods**

**![][image6]**

**createDailyLog()**

* Saves daily progress of a habit  
* Example: user marks habit as completed

**getLogsByHabit()**

* Retrieves logs for a specific habit  
* Used to show progress history


**updateDailyLog()**

* Updates log details such as notes or value  
* Used when user edits past entries

**deleteDailyLog()**

* Removes a log entry  
* Used when incorrect data is entered

**Reminder CRUD Methods**

**![][image7]**

**createReminder()**

* Adds a reminder for a habit

* Example: reminder at 8:00 AM

**getReminders()**

* Retrieves reminders for a habit

* Used to schedule notifications

**updateReminder()**

* Updates reminder time or status

* Used when user changes reminder settings

**deleteReminder()**

* Removes a reminder

* Used when user disables reminders

CRUD method signatures define how the app interacts with the database. They allow the HabitHero app to create, retrieve, update, and delete data. These methods ensure that user actions such as adding habits, tracking progress, and setting reminders are properly stored and managed.

## **5.2 CREATE TABLE SQL**

## **![][assets/screenshots/ERD]**

## **5.3 Migration Strategy**

The HabitHero application uses a database versioning system to manage changes in the database structure. When the database schema needs to be updated, the version number is increased, and the onUpgrade function in the DatabaseHelper class is used to apply the necessary changes. This process ensures that existing user data is preserved while allowing the database to support new features.

The database is currently set to **version 1**. When changes are needed, the version number will be increased (for example, version 2 or version 3). The application detects the version change and automatically runs the upgrade process.

The onUpgrade function updates the database without deleting existing data. Instead of removing tables, the app modifies the database structure by adding new columns or creating new tables when required. This allows the system to introduce new features while keeping all existing records, such as habits, daily logs, and reminders.

The upgrade process is handled step by step. Each version includes specific changes, ensuring compatibility with older data and preventing data loss. By applying updates incrementally, the app provides a smooth and reliable transition when users update HabitHero.

This strategy ensures that the database remains organized, flexible, and safe as the application evolves.
